metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/review-kernel.md` (~line 124) to the doc-update list, updating 'OFF by default (gated on the shared `verify.novelty_drop_active`)' to reflect the new ON-by-default state with explicit-false as the back-out.
findings.finding: docs/review-kernel.md:124 contains stale 'OFF by default (gated on the shared `verify.novelty_drop_active`)' framing that will be left stale after the flip, but the plan's doc-update list does not include this file.
findings.location: What section / docs update list; docs/review-kernel.md:124
findings.suggested_fix: In docs/review-kernel.md near line 124, change 'It is OFF by default (gated on the shared `verify.novelty_drop_active`) and self-gates inert with no prior memory.' to 'It is ON by default (gated on the shared `verify.novelty_drop_active`; set `verify.novelty_drop_active = false` to restore inert behavior) and self-gates inert with no prior memory.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/review-kernel.md` (~line 124, 'OFF by default (gated on the shared verify.novelty_drop_active)') to the docs update list and apply the same default-ON framing + dated operator-authorization note.
findings.finding: docs/review-kernel.md:124 contains 'OFF by default (gated on the shared verify.novelty_drop_active)' — stale framing after the flip — but is not named in the plan's docs update list.
findings.location: What section / docs update list; docs/review-kernel.md:124
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What / Update docs' bullet, append: 'docs/review-kernel.md (~line 124: update "OFF by default" framing to default-ON with explicit-false as the back-out).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py` lines 191-195 (`_maybe_apply_rising_floor` docstring: 'By default the flag is False, so the floor is inert') to the enumerated stale-comment update targets alongside lines 81 and ~523.
findings.finding: src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py lines 191-195 (the _maybe_apply_rising_floor docstring) say 'By default the flag is False, so the floor is inert' — a third stale default-OFF location in that file — but the plan only enumerates lines 81 and ~523 for update.
findings.suggested_fix: Update the docstring to: 'On by default (operator-authorized 2026-07-11); set explicit false in config to restore byte-identical full-review behavior (the back-out). The discriminates_novelty prerequisite was superseded by field evidence.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py` (~line 597, docstring 'OFF by default — same three keys plan-review uses') to the stale-comment update targets and apply default-ON framing.
findings.finding: src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py:597 docstring says 'OFF by default — same three keys plan-review uses' referring to novelty_drop_active — this stale framing is not named in the plan's update list despite the plan explicitly naming :608 for the behavioral gate check.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:194` (the `_maybe_apply_rising_floor` docstring phrase 'By default the flag is False, so the floor is inert') to the stale-docstring update scope in AC5.
findings.finding: The plan's AC5 lists only lines 81 and ~523 in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py as stale docstrings to update, but the docstring at line 194 ('By default the flag is False, so the floor is inert and the verdict is byte-identical to a normal review.') will also be stale after the default flip and is not covered by any AC line.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC5 to read: '…and src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py (lines 81, ~194, ~523).' Update line 194 to: 'Explicit false is the back-out, so the floor is inert and the verdict is byte-identical to a normal review.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the plan's update scope: update the stale 'off-by-default' docstring in `_maybe_apply_rising_floor` (`plan_review/__init__.py:194-195`), the `apply_region_gated_floor` docstring (`workflow_ops.py:597-598`), the inline comment at `workflow_ops.py:608`, and the test section heading at `test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py:189` to reflect that `novelty_drop_active` is now ON by default (explicit false is the back-out).
findings.finding: The plan's update scope for stale 'off-by-default' framing omits three authoritative docstring/comment fragments inside source files that will directly contradict the new True defaults after the flip: (1) `_maybe_apply_rising_floor` docstring at `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:194-195` still says 'By default the flag is False, so the floor is inert'; (2) `apply_region_gated_floor` docstring at `src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py:597-598` says 'OFF by default — same three keys plan-review uses'; (3) inline comment at `workflow_ops.py:608` says 'the shared evidence gate (off by default)'. A fourth stale fragment — the test section heading at `tests/unit/test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py:189` reading '# ── config keys (default-OFF rollout + the back-out) ──────' — will also remain misleading after the assertion flip the plan does name. These are pink-elephant antipatterns: in-code authoritative statements saying the opposite of the new defaults, in the exact code paths that execute the gate.
findings.location: What section / AC bullet listing stale locations: src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:194-195, src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py:597-598 and :608, tests/unit/test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py:189
findings.suggested_fix: In `_maybe_apply_rising_floor` docstring replace 'By default the flag is False, so the floor is inert and the verdict is byte-identical to a normal review.' with 'By default the flag is True (ON); set explicit false to restore byte-identical full-review behavior (the back-out).'. In `apply_region_gated_floor` docstring replace 'OFF by default' with 'ON by default (defaults flipped 2026-07-11; explicit false is the back-out)'. Change inline comment at workflow_ops.py:608 from '# the shared evidence gate (off by default)' to '# the shared evidence gate (on by default since 2026-07-11; explicit false is the back-out)'. Update test section heading at line 189 from 'default-OFF rollout + the back-out' to 'default-ON (flipped 2026-07-11) + the back-out'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC that exercises the newly-defaulted code path end-to-end (e.g., run `rebar review plan <fixture>` with default config and assert the rising-floor / novelty-drop logic engages as expected), or document an explicit rationale for deferring this exercise to the named follow-up task (49ae-258b-4534-4d83).
findings.finding: The plan flips two default flags that activate live code paths — plan-review rising floor (plan_review/__init__.py:204) and code-review region-gated floor (workflow_ops.py:608) — yet no acceptance criterion exercises either path end-to-end in a production-like run. The only ACs for the new behavior are (a) schema default values and (b) unit tests that flip a `is True` assertion; these pass without the floor logic ever firing. An executor could satisfy every AC with the floor logic silently broken and `make test` still green.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — full list; also 'SHARED GATE' bullet in What section
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: '[ ] With default config (no overrides), `rebar review plan tests/fixtures/remediation_round2_plan.yaml` produces a verdict where at least one finding is suppressed by the novelty floor (confirmed via structured output `jq -e ".findings[] | select(.suppressed_by == \"novelty_floor\")"`).' If a suitable fixture does not exist, document deferral to 49ae-258b-4534-4d83 with rationale.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies the code-review region-gated floor is activated by the novelty_drop_active=True default (e.g., assert the relevant code-review unit/integration test exercises the floor path, or that make test covers tests/unit/test_code_review_novelty_floor.py or equivalent).
findings.finding: The What section describes an explicit deliverable — the code-review region-gated floor being activated via the shared novelty_drop_active flag (src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py:608 / gate_dispatch.py:720) — but no AC verifies this code-review floor is exercised or passes correctly. The only behavior-level test AC (AC3) references plan-review triple-gate tests, leaving the code-review side effect untested by any criterion.
findings.location: What section ('SHARED GATE — intentional cross-gate effect') vs Acceptance Criteria (AC3, AC11)
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] The code-review region-gated floor (src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py:608) is exercised with novelty_drop_active=True default; any existing code-review floor tests pass (make test).' This makes the shared-gate scope verifiable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the AC for ADR 0008 and/or ADR 0037 updates to explicitly record that `novelty_drop_active` is a shared gate spanning both plan-review and code-review pipelines, not just a default-flip note — so the cross-component contract is discoverable from either ADR.
findings.finding: The AC for ADR updates only requires a 'dated defaults-flipped ON note' in ADR 0008 and ADR 0037, but neither ADR is asked to explicitly document that novelty_drop_active is a SHARED gate spanning both plan-review and code-review subsystems. A future maintainer reading either ADR in isolation would not learn that toggling this single config key affects two independent review pipelines simultaneously.
findings.location: What section ('SHARED GATE' paragraph) and AC: 'docs/adr/0008 and docs/adr/0037 carry a dated defaults-flipped note'
findings.suggested_fix: In the ADR update instructions, add: 'In at least one of ADR 0008 or ADR 0037 (or a new cross-reference section in both), add a note that novelty_drop_active is a single shared config key gating both plan-review rising-floor (plan_review/__init__.py:204) and code-review region-gated floor (code_review/workflow_ops.py:608), and that any future per-system override must account for this coupling.' The follow-up flag-removal ticket (49ae-258b-4534-4d83) should also reference this shared-gate contract.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a staged rollout note: specify whether a canary run (e.g. 5–10% of tickets, one gate type first) is feasible before flipping both flags globally, or explicitly document why a single 100%-traffic flip is accepted given the cited PASS→BLOCK flip rate.
findings.finding: The plan flips both defaults to True as a single 100%-traffic change with no staged rollout (canary, shadow mode, or percentage ramp). While a cheap rollback exists (set flags to false in config), there is no mechanism to validate behavior on a subset of traffic before full activation — relevant given the cited 38 PASS→BLOCK flips and 32% verdict instability on byte-identical plans.
findings.location: ## What — flag flip description; AC items 1–2
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC item such as: 'Optionally, run a 24h shadow period on ≤10% of plan-review traffic with remediation_mode=True before the full flip, OR document operator sign-off accepting the full-traffic risk given the cited field evidence.' If a full flip is accepted, add a monitoring AC: 'Alert fires if PASS→BLOCK rate exceeds 5% in the first 2h post-deploy.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or name an existing test) that exercises the code-review region-gated floor path (`workflow_ops.py:608` / `gate_dispatch.py:720`) with `novelty_drop_active=True` as the effective default — verifying observable behavior (e.g., a finding is suppressed/retained correctly by the floor) rather than only asserting the config value changed.
findings.finding: The newly-defaulted `novelty_drop_active=True` path through the code-review region-gated floor (`workflow_ops.py:608` via `gate_dispatch.py:720`, per ADR 0037) has no named test coverage in the plan. The only test work named is a config-default assertion flip and plan-review-side tests; `make test` as a pass criterion will go green if the existing suite (written under the False default) exercises this path only incidentally or not at all. This is a cutover that makes the code-review floor the default — offline-green coverage that never exercises that defaulted path end-to-end lets the work close while the live code-review floor path is broken.
findings.location: What section (SHARED GATE bullet) / AC items / tests/unit/test_plan_review_rising_floor.py:166 and test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py:192
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'Existing or new test in tests/unit/test_code_review_novelty_floor.py (or equivalent) exercises the gate_dispatch.py:720 floor path with novelty_drop_active omitted from config (i.e., at its new default True) and asserts the floor suppresses a known-redundant finding.' This confirms the shared gate activates correctly for code-review without requiring a live LLM call.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
